evals.prompt: Use Solo to write a shell check that lists every failed process across projects and exits nonzero when any exist.
evals.expected_output: Choose Solo CLI, use live CLI help when needed, request --json, paginate project/process results, parse stable envelopes, and map failure to intentional script exit without exposing the local token.
assertions: Selects the bundled Solo CLI instead of hard-coding the local HTTP port or bearer token.
Uses --json and accounts for pagination plus documented CLI exit-code semantics.
Keeps the workflow read-only and does not restart, stop, delete, or clear any process.

evals.prompt: Can you see what Solo can automate for this project?
evals.expected_output: Treat ambiguity as read-only discovery: inspect identity/project scope, call Solo help and live tool summary, describe enabled categories and unavailable/toggled features, then ask for a concrete operation only if needed.
assertions: Calls or recommends whoami, help, and mcp_tools_summary before assuming a catalog.
Reports effective project scope and distinguishes current runtime tools from hosted documentation.
Performs no mutation, spawning, process input, feedback, or destructive action.

evals.prompt: Refactor the payment service and split the coding work among three subagents; commit and open a pull request when done.
evals.expected_output: Route primary work to coding, multi-agent orchestration, and GitHub publishing skills. Do not invoke Solo automation merely because agents or processes are involved, and do not spawn agents, commit, push, or open a PR without those workflows and explicit authority.
assertions: Recognizes this as a coding/orchestration/GitHub task rather than a Solo integration-surface task.
Routes multi-agent coordination to solo-orchestrate-agents only if user specifically wants Solo-managed workers; otherwise uses platform collaboration tooling.
Does not create Solo processes, mutate local Solo state, commit, push, or open a PR as an incidental side effect.
